# Accessibility Requirements Checklist: Claude Code Marketplace Installation Path

**Purpose**: Validate that DOC-003 requirements describe an accessible, screen-reader-friendly Claude Code install route with meaningful links, copyable command examples, and no overloaded Claude/Codex comparison tables.
**Created**: 2026-06-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Does the spec require the Claude install route to use descriptive heading hierarchy, lists, and code blocks so screen-reader users can navigate the page and deep-link sections? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec require user-facing command examples to be presented as standalone, copyable code blocks rather than hidden inside dense tables? [Completeness, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK003 Does the spec require link text to identify each destination without relying on surrounding prose such as "here" or "this page"? [Clarity, Spec §FR-017]
- [x] CHK004 Do the requirements explicitly prevent side-by-side or overloaded comparison tables that mix Claude and Codex command forms? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018, Design Concept §Non-goals]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Are the required install, reload, verification, lifecycle, and trust sections named clearly enough to produce stable deep links? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK006 Are the namespaced skill commands required in plain text code formatting so assistive technology users can distinguish literal commands from prose? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK007 Is the Codex boundary clear enough that users do not need to compare Claude and Codex command syntax in the same table or procedure? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012, Spec §FR-018, Plan §Constraints]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK008 Do accessibility expectations align with the docs-only scope rather than implying runtime UI behavior, generated payload changes, or release automation changes? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK009 Do source-backed trust requirements remain compatible with meaningful link text by requiring both official Claude documentation links and repository path references to be identifiable? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-017, Research §Decision: Use official Claude Code docs plus repository source/generated payload citations]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK010 Can a reviewer objectively evaluate whether command examples are copyable and not buried in dense tables? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002, Spec §FR-016]
- [x] CHK011 Can a reviewer objectively evaluate whether links, headings, lists, and code blocks support screen-reader navigation and deep links? [Measurability, Spec §SC-007]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK012 Are accessibility requirements covered for first-time install, verification, lifecycle management, Codex cross-linking, and trust inventory sections? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §User Story 4, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-016, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-018]
- [x] CHK013 Are edge cases covered where dense tables or ambiguous links would make official docs, repository source paths, or generated payload paths hard to identify? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-018]

## Notes

- Checklist focus areas: screen-reader-friendly structure, meaningful link text, standalone copyable command blocks, and avoiding overloaded Claude/Codex comparison tables.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer gate for a docs/process feature.
- Actor/timing: PR reviewer validating accessibility requirements before task generation or implementation.
- Re-run result: CHK001, CHK002, CHK003, CHK004, CHK011, and CHK013 passed after `spec.md` added FR-015 through FR-018, SC-007, and accessibility edge-case assumptions, and `plan.md` pinned the accessible Markdown structure decision.
